scenario: A Camunda 8.8 retailer team configures an HTTP Polling connector to watch a third-party inventory endpoint for 1500 records across its catalog without specifying the optional Interval field. The lead architect wants to confirm the default cadence at which the connector will poll the configured URL before the team picks a custom Interval value for production.
question: What is the documented default polling Interval when the Interval field is left blank on the HTTP Polling connector?

options.text: PT5M as an ISO 8601 duration of 5 minutes between polls of the configured URL endpoint
PT30S as an ISO 8601 duration of 30 seconds between polls of the configured URL endpoint
PT50S as an ISO 8601 duration of 50 seconds between polls of the configured URL endpoint
PT1M as an ISO 8601 duration of 1 minute between polls of the configured URL endpoint

a.text: Incorrect. PT5M is not the documented default for the Interval field; the HTTP Polling connector defaults to 50 seconds, not 5 minutes, so a blank Interval would poll considerably more often than this value.

b.text: Incorrect. PT30S is not the documented default; the HTTP Polling connector defaults to 50 seconds, so leaving the Interval field unset polls less often than every 30 seconds against the URL.

c.text: Correct. The HTTP Polling connector docs state the default Interval is 50 seconds, expressed as the ISO 8601 duration PT50S, applied automatically when the optional Interval field is left unset by the modeler.

d.text: Incorrect. PT1M is not the documented default; the HTTP Polling connector defaults to 50 seconds, which is 10 seconds shorter than a one-minute polling cadence on the configured URL.

explanation: The Interval field on the HTTP Polling connector is optional and accepts ISO 8601 durations; when no value is supplied, the connector polls the configured URL every 50 seconds (PT50S) as the documented default cadence.
